profile.title: The Initiated Reader
profile.summary: Your instincts are already moving beyond surface symbolism. You understand that position shapes meaning, that cards in combination create narrative, and that the useful question is rarely what a card "means" in isolation.
profile.startPoint: Begin directly with spread practice. The Minor Arcana will be your focus — you don't need the Major Arcana foundations.

spread.name: Three Card
spread.description: Past, Present, Future — the spine of any reading.

card.name: The Magician
card.upright: All four suits appear on The Magician's table — cups, wands, swords, pentacles. He has everything he needs. One hand points up, one down: as above, so below. This is the card of directed will — of taking what you have and making something real from it. It asks: what are you capable of that you haven't yet attempted?
card.reversed: Skill used deceptively, or potential squandered. Reversed, The Magician often points to manipulation — someone using their abilities to mislead — or to the querent holding themselves back from their own power.
card.relational: Next to The High Priestess: knowledge and intuition working together, or in tension. Next to The Devil: skill turned to manipulation. Next to The Star: vision finally becoming action.

scenario.love: Someone showing their true capability — or concealing it. The early stage where impressions are everything.
scenario.work: A project where your full skill set is needed. Trust that you have what it takes.
scenario.self: The reminder that capability without application is just potential.

card.name: The Hierophant
card.upright: The Hierophant is the bridge between the divine and the human — a priest, a teacher, an institution. He holds keys to sacred knowledge, but that knowledge comes with doctrine. In a reading he often points to inherited belief systems: religion, culture, professional convention. The question is whether you are following tradition because it serves you, or because you have not yet questioned it.
card.reversed: Breaking from tradition, or tradition that has become oppressive. Reversed, The Hierophant invites radical questioning of received wisdom — or warns that the querent is rejecting structure they actually need.
card.relational: Next to The High Priestess: external doctrine versus inner knowing. Next to The Fool: the tension between convention and the new. Next to The Devil: belief used to control.

scenario.love: A conventional relationship path — marriage, commitment ceremonies. Or the weight of family expectation on a partnership.
scenario.work: Institutional settings, professional bodies, mentors with strict methodologies.
scenario.self: Which of your beliefs actually belong to you?

card.name: The Hermit
card.upright: The Hermit stands on a mountain peak, lantern raised, illuminating only the next few steps. He has withdrawn from the world not in defeat but in search of something that only solitude can deliver. In a reading he calls for a period of genuine reflection — stepping back from noise, from others' opinions, to find your own counsel. The light is yours; it can only illuminate your own path.
card.reversed: Isolation becoming avoidance. Withdrawal that has curdled into disconnection. Or the refusal to spend any time alone — running from the self.
card.relational: Next to The Star: solitude leading toward hope and recovery. Next to The World: the inner journey nearing completion. Next to The Six of Swords: moving away is the right choice right now.

scenario.love: A period of needing space — not rejection, but necessary distance for clarity.
scenario.work: A time for deep focus and single-minded work, away from committees and noise.
scenario.self: What does your own inner voice say when you stop listening to everyone else?
